message:
fix(server/tui): write persisted JSON atomically via temp-file rename

Replaces the direct `std::fs::write(path, json)` calls in the TUI
preference-persistence layer with a new `atomic_write_text` helper so
an interrupted save (crash, OOM kill, disk full, concurrent writer)
never leaves the consumer with a half-written or empty JSON file.

Previously, four call sites (`save_palette_usage`,
`save_dismissed_hints`, `save_screen_filter_presets`, and
`PreferencePersister::export_json`) each did a `create_dir_all` +
`fs::write` pair. If the write started and then the process died, the
on-disk file could be truncated or partially populated, and the next
launch would land on an import error or reset preferences.

The new `atomic_write_text`:

1. Ensures the parent directory exists (falling back to `.` when the
   path has no parent, for safety).
2. Loops up to 64 times trying `.{file_name}.tmp-{pid}-{seq}` names —
   `seq` comes from a new `PERSIST_TMP_SEQ: AtomicU64` so concurrent
   callers within the same process do not collide on the same temp
   path. Temp names start with a leading dot so any hidden-file scan
   skips them.
3. Opens the chosen temp with `create_new(true)` so we only proceed if
   we actually own the file. `AlreadyExists` retries, any other error
   bails out.
4. Writes the contents, calls `sync_data()` to flush to the platform,
   drops the file, and `rename()`s it into place.
5. Special-cases Windows: if the rename fails and the target exists,
   `remove_file` + re-rename to mimic POSIX atomic-replace semantics.
6. On any failure, `remove_file` the temp so leaked `.tmp-*` files
   cannot accumulate even under pathological error paths.
7. If all 64 temp slots are taken (should be impossible under normal
   operation but defends against PID recycling edge cases), returns
   `ErrorKind::AlreadyExists`.

Also removes the now-redundant inline `create_dir_all` from each call
site — the helper takes care of it.

Test coverage
-------------
Adds two regression tests that check both the overwrite path and the
no-temp-leak invariant on the persisted files:

* `export_json_overwrites_existing_file_without_tmp_leaks` — seeds the
  export path with an invalid JSON payload, then calls
  `persister.export_json(&prefs)` and verifies:
    1. the round-tripped import matches `prefs`
    2. the returned path equals `persister.export_path()`
    3. no `.tmp-*` artifact is left in the target directory.

* `palette_usage_save_overwrites_existing_file_without_tmp_leaks` — the
  same invariant for `save_palette_usage`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
